title: MangaKatana
content:
## MangaKatana
### **`IllegalStateException`** / **`This image could not be loaded`** error on some chapter pages:
* The source's Server 1 has issues/is broken, do the following:
  1. Go to your app's Browse page > Extensions tab > MangaKatana settings
  2. Change the Server Preference to `Server 2`
  3. Afterwards, tap "**Clear chapter cache**" in your app's Settings > Data and storage, check if it loads now

title: Mangakakalot, Manganato, Mangabat
content:
## Mangakakalot, Manganato, Mangabat
-# `Last edited: 02/13/2026`
### 1. **`Failed to bypass Cloudflare`**?
* Pass Cloudflare verification in WebView, use the command **`!ua`** in <#1193504423964586025> if the verification loops.
* Or change the mirror URL in the extension's settings (your app's Browse Page > Extensions tab > Mangakakalot > Language settings) to see if that helps.
### 2. **`All CDN attempts failed`**, **`The image could not be loaded`**, and/or downloads are stuck (ex. 16/31) / won't finish (ex. 31/31)?
* The source frequently has missing images on their site, __wait for the source to fix their hosting site/servers__, **this is not an extension-related issue**.
### 3. Chapter page is split into separate pages/cut off?
* That's just how the source cuts them, __not an extension-related issue__.
* Read in long-strip mode or migrate to a different source.
### 4. **`HTTP 429`** error?
* The source has changed their API rate-limit, so update, download, and read less to avoid this error.
* Try changing the mirror URL in the extension's settings to see if it helps.

title: Invalid repo URL error
content:
## **`Invalid repo URL`** error
-# `Last edited: 02/16/2026`
1. Remove the current repo URL from your app & use the alternative repo URL instead:
> ### * https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/keiyoushi/extensions@repo/index.min.json
  * (**`URL do repositório inválido`**) This is especially for users in **South America** since [GitHub randomly blocks South America (**again**)](<https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/177971>) from accessing raw GitHub URLs.
2. Otherwise, switch networks or use a VPN.
